title: AI + Creativity
subtitle: When machines make art — and how humans make it better

diagram.alt: A diffusion model turning a text prompt into an image by gradually removing noise

overview: Welcome to Week 2. You now have the foundation to start creating. Today we explore AI as a creative partner — not a replacement for human creativity, but an amplifier of it.
We will make music, write poetry, generate visual art, and ask the big question: is AI-generated art still "art"?
objectives: Understand how generative AI creates text, images, and music
Use AI tools to create original creative works
Articulate the difference between AI-generated and human creative expression
Develop your own position on authorship and creativity in the AI age
Build a mini creative portfolio using AI collaboration

concepts.heading: How Generative AI Creates
body: Generative AI is trained to create new content rather than just classify or predict. The key technique is called a Diffusion Model (for images) or a Large Language Model / Transformer (for text and music):
bullets: For images (Stable Diffusion, DALL-E, Midjourney): Start with pure noise → the AI gradually de-noises the image, guided by your text prompt, until it produces a coherent picture that matches your description.
For text (GPT-4, Claude): Predict the most likely next token (word fragment) given all previous tokens + training — do this thousands of times to produce a paragraph.

For music (Suno, Udio, MusicGen): Similar to image generation — the AI has learned the patterns of pitch, rhythm, timbre, and structure from millions of songs, and generates new audio that follows these patterns.

concepts.heading: The Creativity Debate
body: Is AI truly creative, or is it just remixing? People take very different positions:

rows: AI has never had an original thought. It recombines patterns from human-created works. True creativity requires consciousness, lived experience, and emotional intent.
"AI creativity is real — the output is new."
If the result is genuinely novel and valuable, does the process matter? Humans also build on existing knowledge and culture. The output is what counts.
The most creative work happens when humans use AI to explore possibilities they couldn't reach alone, then apply judgment, emotion, and intention to shape the result.

concepts.heading: Copyright and Ownership
body: AI-generated content raises complex legal questions that are actively being debated in courts worldwide:
bullets: In Australia, copyright currently requires a human author — AI-only works may not be protected.
If you use AI as a tool and make substantial creative decisions, you may hold copyright over the final work.
Training AI on copyrighted artworks without permission is being challenged in multiple lawsuits globally.
Platform terms vary widely — always check whether you own what you create on any AI platform.

videos.title: How AI Image Generation Works
videos.note: Clear, visual explanation of diffusion models. Perfect intro before the creative activities.

activities.name: AI Creative Workshop
intro: Two creative stations. At Station A you write a song with Suno; at Station B you make visual art with Adobe Firefly. At both, you start with AI and then add your own human touch.
steps: Station A — Visit suno.com (free tier available).
Write a prompt for a song: specify genre, mood, theme, and style. Example: "Upbeat pop song about discovering you can code, energetic, teenage perspective, with a catchy chorus".
Generate the song. Listen carefully. What do you like? What feels off?
Now humanise it: write new lyrics for the chorus yourself. What emotion do YOUR lyrics add that the AI version lacked?
Optional: Generate 3 versions with different genres (pop, hip-hop, classical) of the same theme. How does genre change meaning?
Station B — Visit firefly.adobe.com (free, no age restriction).
Generate an image that represents "the future of education." Use a detailed prompt with style, lighting, and mood.
Generate 4 variations. Select the one that resonates most. Write a sentence explaining why.
Now draw your own version of the same concept — freehand, 5 minutes. Don't try to copy the AI version.
Place both side by side. What did you choose to include that the AI didn't? What did the AI imagine that surprised you?
Reflection: At each station, what did AI do well, and what did you add that made it yours?
facilitator: Set up two stations: Station A (Suno AI for songwriting) and Station B (Adobe Firefly for visual art).
Rotate students through both stations during the 40-minute workshop block.
Emphasise the "humanise it" step — the learning is in what students add, not just what the AI generates.

activities.name: Human vs AI Creative Showdown
intro: Half the class uses AI, half works purely by hand and mind. Then all works are displayed anonymously, and the class tries to guess which are AI and which are human.
steps: Pick ONE challenge as a class: Option A — Write a haiku about loneliness (5-7-5 syllables). Option B — Design a logo for a company called "Future Minds." Option C — Write the opening sentence of a thriller novel set in Sydney in 2040. Option D — Create a short 4-line rhyming verse about why learning matters.
You have 5 minutes to complete the challenge.
Display every work anonymously — no names attached.
Vote on which pieces you think are AI and which are human.
Reveal the answers and discuss: what gave the AI ones away? What surprised you?
facilitator: Choose ONE creative challenge from the options. Everyone — students AND facilitator — has 5 minutes to complete it.
Half the class uses AI. Half does it purely by hand/mind. Then display all works anonymously.
Class tries to guess which are AI and which are human. Vote, reveal, discuss.

reflection: When you collaborated with AI on a creative project today, at what point did the work start to feel like "yours"? What creative decisions did you make?
Do you think AI-generated art should be eligible for art prizes? What criteria would you use to judge?
If AI can write songs, draw illustrations, and compose poetry — what remains uniquely human about creativity?
Build your Creative + AI project plan: What creative project would you like to work on for your final showcase? How will AI assist, and what will you contribute that AI cannot?

vocab.definition: AI that creates new content (images, text, audio, video).
AI architecture that generates images by reversing a noise process.
AI trained on vast text corpora to generate and understand language.
The basic unit an LLM processes — roughly a word fragment.
A detailed description of the creative output you want the AI to generate.
Legal right protecting original creative works from unauthorised copying.
AI architecture using competing generator and discriminator networks.
The mathematical space inside an AI where concepts are stored as relationships between numbers.

quiz.q: How does a diffusion model create an image from your text prompt?
options: It copies the closest matching image from its training set
It starts with pure noise and gradually removes it, guided by your prompt, until a clear picture appears
It photographs a real scene and edits it
It draws the image pixel by pixel from the top-left corner
quiz.explain: A diffusion model begins with random noise and de-noises it step by step, using your text prompt as a guide, until a coherent image that matches your description emerges.

quiz.q: When an LLM like GPT-4 or Claude writes a paragraph, what is it actually doing?
options: Looking up the answer in a database
Predicting the most likely next token thousands of times in a row
Translating your prompt into another language and back
Asking a human to write each sentence
quiz.explain: Text generators work token by token (roughly word-fragment by word-fragment), predicting the most likely next token given everything before it — repeated thousands of times to build a paragraph.

quiz.q: In Australia today, an artwork generated entirely by AI with no human author is automatically protected by copyright.
quiz.explain: False. Australian copyright currently requires a human author, so AI-only works may not be protected. If you make substantial creative decisions, though, you may hold copyright over the final work.

quiz.q: Which statement best matches the "Human + AI is the real breakthrough" view in the creativity debate?
options: AI should never be used in art at all
Only the final output matters, so the process is irrelevant
The best work comes when humans use AI to explore further, then add judgment, emotion, and intention
AI is always more creative than any human
quiz.explain: This perspective says the magic is in collaboration: AI helps humans reach ideas they couldn't alone, and then the human shapes the result with judgment, emotion, and intention.

quiz.q: Music generators like Suno create songs by learning patterns of pitch, rhythm, timbre, and structure from huge numbers of existing songs.
quiz.explain: True. Like image generation, music AI learns the patterns of pitch, rhythm, timbre, and structure from millions of songs, then generates new audio that follows those patterns.
